name: Corned Beef with Sweet-Hot Glaze
recipeIngredient: 1 Corned beef round or center-cut brisket, fat trimmed (3.5 to 4 lb.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the meat well with cool water. Place in a 6- to 8-quart pan with about 3 quarts of water. Bring to a boil over high heat; drain. Repeat this step until the water no longer tastes salty, 1 or 2 more times. To the drained meat, add water (about 2 quarts) to cover it by about 1/2". Bring to boiling on high heat; cover and simmer gently until the meat is very tender when pierced, about 3 1/2 hours.
2. Drain the beef; put in a 9x13" pan. If made ahead, cool, then chill an airtight container up until the next day. Cover the meat tightly with foil; bake in a 350°F oven until hot in the center, about 50 minutes; uncover.
3. In the pan used to simmer beef, mix broth, sugar, and horseradish. Boil over high heat until the glaze is reduced to 1/4 cup, about 5 minutes; stir often. If made ahead, pour into a bowl and cover; chill up until the next day.
4. Bake hot meat, uncovered, in a 350°F oven for 20 minutes, brushing with glaze until all is used. Broil about 6 inches from the heat until the top browns lightly, 3-5 minutes. Put the meat on a platter; offer mustard.
